reasoning:

Despite this outsized drop, historical trend analysis and standard post-selloff reversion points to a positive expectation for recovery (the yfinance tool estimates P(>26.63) at 0.60 over a short horizon). Management also maintained their $0.70 quarterly dividend, signaling that the company expects underlying cashflow resilience despite the chaotic equity pricing. 

Given extreme ongoing volatility—with daily swings of up to 15%—the chance of DOW resolving above the 26.63 mark in the immediate weeks is relatively favorable as it looks to bounce from oversold territory. However, in adhering to the instruction that uncertainty increases over time resulting in probabilities closer to 0.50, and given that DOW will experience at least two $0.70 structural downward price adjustments on ex-dividend dates before October, the baseline probabilities are smoothly adjusted downward toward a coin-toss for the longest forecast horizon.
